skill.name: crossref-database
skill.description: Access CrossRef metadata for scholarly works; use when you need to resolve a DOI or search CrossRef to retrieve bibliographic details, citation/reference counts, or funder information for research and citation management.
skill.summary: Resolve DOIs and search CrossRef metadata to retrieve bibliographic details, citation counts, and funder information for academic research.
skill.author: AIPOCH

security_audit.summary: All 41 static analysis findings are false positives. The 6 'Weak cryptographic algorithm' findings are triggered by the word 'CrossRef' containing 'crypt'. The 30 'Ruby/shell backtick execution' findings are standard markdown fenced code blocks displaying bash commands as documentation only. The 3 'System reconnaissance' and 2 'Hardcoded URL' findings reference legacy audit notes and a legitimate source attribution link. This skill performs DOI resolution and metadata queries against the legitimate CrossRef REST API using the habanero Python library with no command injection vectors or unsafe operations.

content.user_title: Resolve DOIs and search scholarly metadata with CrossRef
content.value_statement: Researchers need authoritative bibliographic metadata to verify citations and assess research impact. This skill resolves DOIs and searches CrossRef to provide titles, authors, citation counts, and funder information from publisher-verified records.
actual_capabilities: Resolve a DOI to full bibliographic metadata including title, authors, publication venue, and date
Search CrossRef using keywords to find scholarly works and their DOIs
Retrieve reference counts and cited-by counts for impact assessment
Extract funder information associated with publications
Support citation management workflows with authoritative publisher data
Validate the skill environment with built-in helper scripts
limitations: Citation count and funder data availability depends on what publishers deposit into CrossRef
Search results are ranked by CrossRef relevance which may not match user priorities
Requires Python and the habanero library to be installed in the environment
Does not provide access to full-text articles or subscription-only content

use_cases.title: Verify citation metadata for manuscript references
use_cases.description: An academic researcher uses DOI resolution to verify author names, publication years, and journal titles for all citations in a manuscript before submission.

use_cases.title: Bibliographic reference checking for library collections
use_cases.description: A librarian searches CrossRef by keywords and author names to find correct DOIs and bibliographic details for catalog records and reference checks.

use_cases.title: Compile citation impact data for research assessment
use_cases.description: A research analyst collects reference counts and cited-by counts for a set of publications to evaluate research output impact for institutional reporting.

prompt_templates.title: Resolve a single DOI to bibliographic metadata
prompt_templates.prompt: Resolve the DOI 10.1371/journal.pone.0029797 using the crossref-database skill. Return the title, authors, publication date, journal name, and DOI.
prompt_templates.scenario: Getting bibliographic details for a known DOI

prompt_templates.title: Search CrossRef by keyword for relevant works
prompt_templates.prompt: Use the crossref-database skill to search for works related to 'climate change adaptation' with a limit of 10 results. List each result with its title, authors, publication year, and DOI.
prompt_templates.scenario: Finding scholarly works on a topic

prompt_templates.title: Retrieve citation impact metrics for a publication
prompt_templates.prompt: Use the crossref-database skill to resolve DOI 10.1038/s41586-020-2649-2. Include the reference count and cited-by count in the output to assess citation impact.
prompt_templates.scenario: Evaluating citation metrics for research assessment

prompt_templates.title: Extract funder information from multiple publications
prompt_templates.prompt: Use the crossref-database skill to resolve these DOIs: 10.1371/journal.pone.0029797 and 10.1038/s41586-020-2649-2. For each publication, extract the funder information if available and present it in a comparison table.
prompt_templates.scenario: Identifying funding sources for multiple publications

output_examples.output:
Title: 'Experimental evidence for the species-area relationship in a tropical forest'
Authors: John Terborgh, Kenneth Feeley, Miles Silman, et al.
Publication Date: 2012
Journal: PLOS ONE
Publisher: Public Library of Science
DOI: 10.1371/journal.pone.0029797
Reference Count: 47
Cited-by Count: 12

output_examples.input: Search CrossRef for works on 'machine learning in healthcare' (limit 3)
output_examples.output:
Search results for 'machine learning in healthcare':

1. 'A survey of machine learning applications in healthcare' (2023) - DOI: 10.xxxx/example1
   Authors: Smith J, Patel R
   Journal: Journal of Medical Informatics

2. 'Deep learning for medical image analysis' (2024) - DOI: 10.xxxx/example2
   Authors: Chen L, Wang Y
   Journal: Nature Digital Medicine

3. 'Machine learning clinical decision support' (2023) - DOI: 10.xxxx/example3
   Authors: Kumar A, Jones B
   Journal: Lancet Digital Health

best_practices: Provide a complete DOI including the doi.org prefix for reliable resolution with full metadata
Use specific search queries with author surnames and key title words to improve result relevance
Run the validation command before full execution to confirm the Python environment and habanero library are ready
anti_patterns: Using the skill to fabricate bibliographic data for non-existent publications or fake citations
Searching with overly broad single-word queries that return hundreds of irrelevant results
Attempting to resolve paywalled content or full-text articles through CrossRef which only provides metadata

faq.question: What data does CrossRef provide?
faq.answer: CrossRef provides bibliographic metadata including titles, authors, publication dates, journal names, publishers, DOIs, reference counts, and funder information when available.

faq.question: Can I access full-text articles through this skill?
faq.answer: No. CrossRef is a metadata registry and does not provide full-text article content. This skill only retrieves bibliographic metadata and citation counts.

faq.question: What is a DOI and how do I use it?
faq.answer: A Digital Object Identifier (DOI) is a permanent identifier for a publication. You provide the DOI string (e.g., 10.1371/journal.pone.0029797) and the skill resolves it to retrieve the associated metadata.

faq.question: Do I need an API key to use CrossRef?
faq.answer: No. The CrossRef REST API is free and open. The skill uses the habanero Python library which works without authentication for standard queries.

faq.question: What is the habanero library?
faq.answer: Habanero is a Python client library for the CrossRef API. It must be installed with 'pip install habanero' before using the skill scripts.

faq.question: How accurate are the citation counts from CrossRef?
faq.answer: Citation counts depend on what publishers deposit into CrossRef. They reflect cross-references within the CrossRef network and may not include all citations found in other databases.
